---
title: "Datasets"
author: "Ronnie Bailey-Steinitz"
date: "2026-05-15"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Producing Gorilla Dataset for Week 1

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Create faux gorilla hormone + behavioral dataset
# using real gorilla names, sex, and first observed group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(janitor)

set.seed(123)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Read real gorilla names, sex, and first observed group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gorilla_pool <- readr::read_csv("Logistics/gorilla_names_groups.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    gorilla_name = full_name,
    group = first_obs_group
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    gorilla_name = stringr::str_squish(gorilla_name),
    sex = stringr::str_squish(sex),
    group = stringr::str_squish(group)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(gorilla_name),
    gorilla_name != "",
    !is.na(group),
    group != "",
    sex %in% c("M", "F")
  )

# quick checks
dplyr::glimpse(gorilla_pool)
dplyr::count(gorilla_pool, sex)
dplyr::count(gorilla_pool, group, sort = TRUE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Select individuals for faux dataset
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gorillas <- gorilla_pool %>%
  dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::n() >= 4) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::sample_n(size = 30) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    gorilla_id = paste0("G", stringr::str_pad(dplyr::row_number(), width = 3, pad = "0")),
    
    age_years = round(runif(dplyr::n(), min = 3, max = 38), 1),
    
    age_class = dplyr::case_when(
      age_years < 4 ~ "infant",
      age_years < 8 ~ "juvenile",
      age_years < 12 ~ "subadult",
      TRUE ~ "adult"
    ),
    
    dominance_rank = dplyr::case_when(
      age_class != "adult" ~ NA_real_,
      sex == "M" ~ sample(1:4, size = dplyr::n(), replace = TRUE),
      sex == "F" ~ sample(2:8, size = dplyr::n(), replace = TRUE)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    gorilla_id,
    gorilla_name,
    group,
    sex,
    age_years,
    age_class,
    dominance_rank
  )

# group size based on selected individuals
group_sizes <- gorillas %>%
  dplyr::count(group, name = "group_size")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Create repeated sample-level dataset
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gorilla_hormones <- gorillas %>%
  dplyr::left_join(group_sizes, by = "group") %>%
  dplyr::slice(rep(1:dplyr::n(), each = 8)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(gorilla_id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    sample_number = dplyr::row_number(),
    sample_id = paste0(gorilla_id, "_S", stringr::str_pad(sample_number, 2, pad = "0")),
    sample_date = as.Date("2025-01-01") + sample(0:364, size = dplyr::n(), replace = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    observer = sample(
      c("Robert", "Paul", "Mark", "Sarah", "Mary"),
      size = dplyr::n(),
      replace = TRUE
    ),
    
    fruit_availability_index = dplyr::case_when(
      lubridate::month(sample_date) %in% c(1, 2, 3, 10, 11, 12) ~ rnorm(dplyr::n(), mean = 0.70, sd = 0.12),
      TRUE ~ rnorm(dplyr::n(), mean = 0.40, sd = 0.12)
    ),
    
    fruit_availability_index = pmin(pmax(fruit_availability_index, 0.05), 0.95),
    
    feeding_time_prop = dplyr::case_when(
      group_size >= 4 ~ rbeta(dplyr::n(), shape1 = 7, shape2 = 5),
      group_size == 3 ~ rbeta(dplyr::n(), shape1 = 6, shape2 = 5),
      group_size == 2 ~ rbeta(dplyr::n(), shape1 = 5, shape2 = 6),
      TRUE ~ rbeta(dplyr::n(), shape1 = 4, shape2 = 7)
    ),
    
    aggression_events = rpois(
      dplyr::n(),
      lambda = dplyr::case_when(
        sex == "M" & age_class == "adult" ~ 3.0,
        sex == "M" ~ 1.8,
        TRUE ~ 0.8
      )
    ),
    
    parasite_load = rnbinom(
      dplyr::n(),
      size = 2,
      mu = dplyr::case_when(
        age_class %in% c("infant", "juvenile") ~ 35,
        age_class == "subadult" ~ 25,
        TRUE ~ 18
      )
    ),
    
    respiratory_signs = rbinom(
      dplyr::n(),
      size = 1,
      prob = dplyr::case_when(
        group_size >= 4 ~ 0.25,
        TRUE ~ 0.12
      )
    ),
    
    injury_status = rbinom(
      dplyr::n(),
      size = 1,
      prob = dplyr::case_when(
        aggression_events >= 3 ~ 0.22,
        TRUE ~ 0.06
      )
    ),
    
    cortisol_ng_ml = exp(
      2.8 +
        0.08 * aggression_events +
        0.35 * respiratory_signs +
        0.25 * injury_status -
        0.35 * feeding_time_prop +
        rnorm(dplyr::n(), mean = 0, sd = 0.35)
    ),
    
    testosterone_ng_ml = exp(
      1.0 +
        ifelse(sex == "M", 1.0, 0) +
        ifelse(age_class == "adult", 0.5, 0) +
        ifelse(sex == "M" & !is.na(dominance_rank), -0.12 * dominance_rank, 0) +
        0.05 * aggression_events +
        rnorm(dplyr::n(), mean = 0, sd = 0.45)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(
    sample_id,
    gorilla_id,
    gorilla_name,
    group,
    sample_date,
    observer,
    sex,
    age_class,
    age_years,
    dominance_rank,
    group_size,
    fruit_availability_index,
    feeding_time_prop,
    aggression_events,
    parasite_load,
    respiratory_signs,
    injury_status,
    cortisol_ng_ml,
    testosterone_ng_ml
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Inspect and save dataset
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dplyr::glimpse(gorilla_hormones)

summary(gorilla_hormones)

readr::write_csv(gorilla_hormones, "gorilla_hormone_behavior_data.csv")

# save as CSV
# readr::write_csv(gorilla_hormones, "gorilla_hormone_behavior_data.csv")
```

repeated measures by gorilla_id
continuous variables: cortisol_ng_ml, testosterone_ng_ml, age_years, fruit_availability_index
count variables: aggression_events, parasite_load
binary variables: respiratory_signs, injury_status
categorical variables: group, sex, age_class, observer
proportion variable: feeding_time_prop

```{r}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Testosterone differs strongly by sex
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = sex, y = testosterone_ng_ml, fill = sex)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Testosterone differs by sex",
    x = "Sex",
    y = "Testosterone (ng/mL)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Cortisol increases with aggression
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = aggression_events, y = cortisol_ng_ml)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Cortisol increases with aggression",
    x = "Aggression events",
    y = "Cortisol (ng/mL)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Feeding time proportion differs across groups
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = group, y = feeding_time_prop, fill = group)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Feeding time proportion differs across groups",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Feeding time proportion"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45)
  ) 
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Younger gorillas tend to have higher parasite loads
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = age_years, y = parasite_load, color = sex)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Parasite load declines with age",
    x = "Age (years)",
    y = "Parasite load"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Respiratory signs are more common in larger groups
# ------------------------------------------------------------

resp_summary <- gorilla_hormones %>%
  dplyr::group_by(group, group_size) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    respiratory_prop = mean(respiratory_signs),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot2::ggplot(
  resp_summary,
  ggplot2::aes(x = group_size, y = respiratory_prop)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Respiratory signs are more common in larger groups",
    x = "Group size",
    y = "Proportion with respiratory signs"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Adult males show the highest testosterone values
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = age_class, y = testosterone_ng_ml, fill = sex)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Adult males show the highest testosterone values",
    x = "Age class",
    y = "Testosterone (ng/mL)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Cortisol decreases as feeding time increases
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = feeding_time_prop, y = cortisol_ng_ml)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Cortisol decreases with feeding time",
    x = "Feeding time proportion",
    y = "Cortisol (ng/mL)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Hormone distributions are strongly right-skewed
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot2::ggplot(
  gorilla_hormones,
  ggplot2::aes(x = cortisol_ng_ml)
) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Distribution of cortisol values",
    x = "Cortisol (ng/mL)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. QQ plot for cortisol
# ------------------------------------------------------------

qqnorm(gorilla_hormones$cortisol_ng_ml)
qqline(gorilla_hormones$cortisol_ng_ml)
```
